name: Currais e Calçada Trail
name_original: Trilho dos Currais e Calçada — percurso circular na Serra de Montejunto
region: Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal
description: Jog the moderate 5.2-mile Trilho dos Currais e Calçada circular route in Serra de Montejunto, featuring historical stone structures and pano
region_intro: Jogging around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal offers diverse landscapes within the Alentejo Litoral region of Portugal. The area is characterized by the Serra do Cercal mountain range, which reaches 372 meters at its highest point, offering varied terrain from flattened relief to cork oak forests. Joggers can explore traditional Alentejo landscapes with small farms, orchards, and fields, alongside routes that provide access to the nearby Alentejo coast. The region's trail networks include sections of the Rota Vicentina, providing options for different running experiences. Best jogging routes around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal The most popular jogging route is Serra de Montejunto Windmills Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Serra de Montejunto. Another top favourite among local runners is Quinta da Serra Trail, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This route offers a shorter option with less elevation, suitable for a quicker run. Local runners also love the Currais e Calçada Trail, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Jogging around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal is defined by mountain ranges, traditional agricultural landscapes, and proximity to coastal areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a couple of reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Lamas e Cercal area?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 10 running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal, offering a variety of experiences for joggers. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Lamas e Cercal?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from flattened relief to cork oak forests within the Serra do Cercal mountain range. You'll encounter traditional Alentejo landscapes with small farms, orchards, and fields, as well as routes providing access to the nearby Alentejo coast. The trails include sections of the renowned Rota Vicentina, ensuring varied surfaces suitable for running.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced joggers?
A: Yes, the area features several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381122847/serra-de-montejunto-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montejunto Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6.2-mile (10.0 km) route with over 440 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meia Encosta Trail – Chapel of Saint John the Baptist loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 15 miles (25 km) with substantial elevation changes.

Q: Can I find any moderate or easier running paths in the region?
A: Absolutely. For a quicker run with less elevation, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381133431/quinta-da-serra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta da Serra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.0-mile (4.9 km) path. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381132483/currais-e-calcada-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Currais e Calçada Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 5.2 miles (8.3 km) through varied terrain.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I might see while jogging?
A: While running, you can encounter several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montejunto Watchtower&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/760297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; are a notable man-made monument. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ota River&lt;/a&gt; or the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1760096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Our Lady of the Snows&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's natural beauty and history.

Q: Are there any circular or loop running routes in Lamas e Cercal?
A: Yes, the area is known for its circular routes that showcase the harmonious coexistence of man and nature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381136513/carreiro-dos-ss-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carreiro dos SS Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.3-mile (8.5 km) difficult route that offers a complete circuit. The region also features trails like the "Cercal do Alentejo – Alentejo Manor loop," which provides a moderate 7.54 km loop experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in this region?
A: The spring months are particularly pleasant for outdoor activities in Cercal do Alentejo, with blooming flowers and mild temperatures. The temperate climate of the Alentejo Litoral region generally makes it suitable for jogging for much of the year, but spring offers an especially vibrant and comfortable experience.

Q: How can I access the running trails, particularly regarding parking or public transport?
A: While specific parking details for each route vary, many trails in the Cercal do Alentejo area are accessible from local villages. The region is part of the Southwest Alentejo and Vicentine Coast Natural Park, and trails often lead through agricultural lands, suggesting local access points. For public transport, researching local bus services to Cercal do Alentejo would be advisable, though direct access to trailheads might require a short walk or taxi.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Lamas e Cercal?
A: The trails in the Cercal do Alentejo area, being part of a natural park and agricultural landscapes, are generally welcoming to dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through farmlands or protected natural areas, to respect local wildlife and livestock.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Lamas e Cercal?
A: The running routes in Lamas e Cercal e Cercal e Cercal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from numerous reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from mountain ranges to traditional agricultural landscapes, and the proximity to coastal areas, offering diverse and scenic experiences.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes near the running routes?
A: Cercal do Alentejo, as a local hub, offers cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your run. Many trails pass through or near villages, providing opportunities for refreshments. Additionally, facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5706330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejunto Rural Campsite&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; offer spots for a break.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options available in the region?
A: Yes, the region is home to sections of the famous Rota Vicentina, a network of long-distance trails. This includes the "Caminho Histórico" and the "Trilho dos Pescadores," which offer extensive routes suitable for long-distance running. The "Cercal do Alentejo – Porto Covo" section is one such example, providing a substantial challenge for endurance runners.
tips: It is located in Picoto do Castelo, close to Pragança, on the northwest slope of the Serra de Montejunto. It was discovered in 1893 and since then has been the subject of continued study. Traces found in the area indicate that it was a settlement occupied since the Late Neolithic, through the Copper, Bronze and Iron ages, and finally Romanized, with signs of occupation until the 1st century. several national museums, being considered very important for the study of Prehistory, Protohistory and the Romanization of the country. Castro de Pragança is currently in quite abandoned condition.

Located in the heart of the Montejunto mountain range, the Montejunto Rural Camping Park has ample shade and capacity for 90 people. Free to use, it has a bar, a tourist reception center and an Environmental Interpretation Centre.

https://montejunto.pt/parque-de-campismo-rural-da-serra-de-montejunto/

Located in the chestnut woods, it connects the Real Fábrica do Gelo complex and the Environmental Interpretation Centre. The Park has eight tables, with an average capacity of six to eight people per table. As support structures for its use, it has a small bar, toilets and a small playground.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Trilho dos Currais e Calçada?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this route aren't detailed, the trail is located within the Serra de Montejunto. You might find parking near the Montejunto Rural Campsite or the Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto, which are points of interest along or near the route.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Serra de Montejunto Protected Landscape. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always carry bags to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Montejunto has a distinct microclimate. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is recommended. Winters are generally mild but can be wet, so appropriate gear is essential.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the Serra de Montejunto Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Paisagem Protegida da Serra de Montejunto, including the Trilho dos Currais e Calçada. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of forest roads and pastoral trails. While classified as moderate, it includes significant ascents and descents, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness in certain sections. You'll encounter unique geological formations and historical stone structures.

faqs.question: What unique natural features or historical landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll jog through areas with unique geological formations called "covões" (depressions), often associated with "currais" (small stone houses or enclosures) and delineated by "calçadas" (limestone walls). These are remnants of traditional farming. The Serra de Montejunto itself offers spectacular panoramic views and a rich biodiversity.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by or near several interesting spots. You can explore the Castro de Pragança, a historical site. There's also the Montejunto Rural Campsite and the Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto, which could be useful for a break.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in Serra de Montejunto?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find parts of the Trilho da Quinta da Serra, the Estação da Biodiversidade de Montejunto, and the [CIMA PR06] Montejunto * Pragança integrated into its path.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 8.3 kilometers long. For jogging, it typically takes around 1 hour and 7 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring appropriate jogging shoes with good grip, sufficient water, and snacks. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is recommended, especially during warmer months. A light jacket might be useful due to the microclimate, and a fully charged phone for navigation and emergencies is always a good idea.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll find the Montejunto Rural Campsite and the Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto, which can offer basic facilities. For more extensive amenities like restaurants or shops, you would need to visit nearby towns such as Cadaval or Alenquer.

waypoints.name: Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto
